data.text: In objective list on question practice, remove loading state.
The user wants to remove the loading state from the objective list on the question practice page. Let me first explore the codebase to find the relevant files.

input.prompt: Search the codebase to find files related to "objective list" on the "question practice" page. Look for loading states (skeletons, spinners, loading indicators) in components that display objectives. Check both frontend/src/pages/ and frontend/src/components/ directories. Return the full file paths, line numbers of loading states, and the surrounding code context (about 20 lines around each loading state).

state.output:
Here are my complete findings: --- ## Files Related to "Objective List" on the Question Practice Page The "question practice" page is the **LessonPage** component. It renders an objective list via the **ObjectiveTracker** component. There are three key files: --- ### 1. `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/src/pages/lesson-page.tsx` This is the main "question practice" page. It contains multiple loading states: **Loading State #1 -- Page-level loading spinner (lines 283-289)** When the page first mounts and the plan is being fetched: ```tsx if (phase === 'loading') { return (
) } ``` **Loading State #2 -- Quiz generation spinner (lines 371-386)** When the current quiz is being lazily generated (the `loadingQuiz` flag), displayed in the main content area instead of the MCQWidget: ```tsx

{currentSlot ? ( <> Generating question {currentSlot.questionIndex + 1} for path {currentSlot.objectiveIndex + 1}... ) : ( <>Wrapping up... )}

``` **Loading State #3 -- Finishing/summary generation (lines 387-392)** When the attempt is being finalized into a progress report: ```tsx {finishing && (

Building your progress report...

)} ``` The `ObjectiveTracker` component is rendered at **line 396** in the `
` sidebar. It does NOT have a skeleton/loading state itself -- it always renders with whatever data it receives. --- ### 2. `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/src/components/objective-tracker.tsx` This is the component that displays the objective list. It has one loading indicator: **Loading State #4 -- "Current" objective spinner (lines 58-61)** When an objective's status is `'current'` (actively being worked on), a `Loader2` spinning icon is shown next to it: ```tsx ) : status === 'current' ? ( ``` This is the only loading indicator within the objective list itself. There are **no skeleton states** in this component. The component does not have any conditional loading -- it renders the full list immediately with whatever `statuses` array is passed to it. --- ### 3. `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/src/components/mcq-widget.tsx` This is the question widget that sits alongside the objective tracker. It has multiple loading states: **Loading State #5 -- Submit button spinner (line 209)** ```tsx {submitting ? : } ``` **Loading State #6 -- Hint request button spinner (line 213)** ```tsx {hintLoading ? : } ``` **Loading State #7 -- Explanation loading (lines 250-251)** ```tsx {explanationLoading ? ( ``` **Loading State #8 -- AI Help "Thinking..." indicator (lines 348-352)** Inside the `LearnMorePanel` chat overlay: ```tsx {loading && (
Thinking...
)} ``` --- ### 4. `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/src/components/plan-card.tsx` Displayed during the "review" phase (before quiz practice). Has loading states for the approve and revise buttons: **Loading State #9 -- Regenerate button spinner (lines 180-182)** ```tsx Revising... ``` **Loading State #10 -- Approve button spinner (lines 193-195)** ```tsx Approving... ``` --- ### 5. `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/src/components/ui/skeleton.tsx` A reusable `Skeleton` component exists (using `animate-pulse`) but is **NOT used anywhere** in the objective-related components. It is only defined in this file: ```tsx function Skeleton({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<'div'>) { return
} ``` --- ## Summary | File | Line(s) | Loading Type | What It Indicates | |------|---------|-------------|-------------------| | `lesson-page.tsx` | 283-289 | `Loader2 animate-spin` | Initial page load (fetching plan) | | `lesson-page.tsx` | 371-386 | `Loader2 animate-spin` in Card | Generating next quiz question | | `lesson-page.tsx` | 387-392 | `Loader2 animate-spin` | Building progress report | | `objective-tracker.tsx` | 58-61 | `Loader2 animate-spin` | Current objective in progress | | `mcq-widget.tsx` | 209 | `Loader2 animate-spin` in Button | Submitting answer | | `mcq-widget.tsx` | 213 | `Loader2 animate-spin` in Button | Requesting hint | | `mcq-widget.tsx` | 250-251 | `Loader2 animate-spin` in Button | Loading explanation | | `mcq-widget.tsx` | 348-352 | `Loader2 animate-spin` | AI chat "Thinking..." | | `plan-card.tsx` | 180-182 | `Loader2 animate-spin` in Button | Revising/regenerating plan | | `plan-card.tsx` | 193-195 | `Loader2 animate-spin` in Button | Approving plan | **Key observation:** The objective list in `objective-tracker.tsx` has **no skeleton loading state**. It always renders immediately. The only loading indicator within it is the spinner icon for the currently active objective. The `Skeleton` component (`ui/skeleton.tsx`) exists in the codebase but is not used anywhere in the objective-related components.

state.title: frontend/src/components/objective-tracker.tsx

data.text:
The user wants to remove the loading state from the objective list. The loading state is the `Loader2` spinning icon shown for the "current" status (lines 58-61). I need to replace it with a different icon - likely a filled circle or similar indicator to show it's the current objective, just without the spinning animation.

Looking at the pattern, I should replace the `Loader2` spinner with a static indicator. A filled circle (like a dot) would make sense to indicate the current objective without animation.
I'll replace the spinning `Loader2` icon for the `current` status with a static filled circle indicator.
